rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Bradshaw And Associates Pcs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents Set Two To Defendants Special Interrogatories Set Two And To Compel Production Of Documents From Plaintiff Leslie
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents Set Two To Defendants Special Interrogatories Set Two And To Compel Production Of Documents From Plaintiff Leslie Finley. Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH ANDREWS VS. DAVID B. RICKEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT DAVID RICKEY'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Denied. Plaintiff met his burden of establishing that personal service was properly effectuated. Evidence Code sec. 647 provides a presumption that the facts stated in the proof of service by a registered process server are true. (See American Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, holding that "[b]ecause of the statutory presumption, defendant was thus required to produce evidence that he was not served.") Here, Defendant has not produced sufficient evidence to overcome the presumption that he was personally served. The Declaration of Miles Pawski states that Defendant returns from Haiti to San Francisco "periodically" and that he has resided at the address where the purported personal service occurred. (Declaration of Miles Pawski, pars. 7-8.) Although Mr. Pawski states in a conclusory fashion that Defendant did not reside there on the date of service, Mr. Pawski provides no facts to support this contention. (Id. at par. 7.) The only specific fact provided about the address is that a tenant by the name of Mr. McCallum moved into the property in December 2014. (Id.) This fact does not establish that Defendant did not reside at the property on the date of service in August 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: CARISSA COPELAND VS. JOHN VINTON GILMOUR IV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT JOHN GILMOUR, SARAH LOVE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. The motion to quash service is granted. To effectuate substitute service: a copy of the summons and complaint must be left at the individual's dwelling house ("usual place of abode") with a "competent member of the household" or person "apparently in charge of his or her office, place of business, or usual mailing address," at least 18 years old, who must be told what the papers are. [See CCP § 415.20(b).] The person with whom the papers are left need not be a member of the family. [See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 CA4th 1387, 1393.] Here, it is undisputed that Plaintiff served the summons and complaint on Mr. Joshua Wagner, at 650 Alhambra Street in Crockett, CA, on January 18, 2015. However, 650 Alhambra Street is not Defendant's dwelling house under CCP § 415.20(b). Therefore the Court finds the substitute service to be invalid. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: SWINERTON BUILDERS VS. SAN FRANCISCO FILIPINO CULTURAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against San Francisco Filipino Cultural Center, Llc And Its Counsel, Victor Marquez
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SWINERTON BUILDERS' Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against San Francisco Filipino Cultural Center, LLC And Its Counsel, Victor Marquez (Part 1 of 2 For Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling Only). Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Swinerton Builders moves to compel defendant San Francisco Filipino Cultural Center, LLC, to provide further answers to its interrogatories, and for sanctions against defendant. The Court having considered the papers on the motion, the arguments of counsel, and the law, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court rules as follows: 1. It appears to the Court that, with its Supplemental Responses to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One, defendant has provided the answers sought by plaintiff. For this reason, plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Answers to Form Interrogatories is denied as moot. (Continued in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SWINERTON BUILDERS VS. SAN FRANCISCO FILIPINO CULTURAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against San Francisco Filipino Cultural Center, Llc And Its Counsel, Victor Marquez (Part 2 Of 2 For The Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SWINERTON BUILDERS' Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against San Francisco Filipino Cultural Center, Llc And Its Counsel, Victor Marquez (Part 2 Of 2 For The Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only). (Continued From Part 1 of 2) 2. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is also denied. It appears that defendant's counsel was not aware that plaintiff viewed defendant's original response to plaintiff's interrogatories as deficient; when plaintiff's counsel wrote to complain about the original response on June 1, 2015, defendant's counsel was out of the country for several weeks. Although defendant's counsel could not abandon his responsibilities when he left the country, plaintiff's counsel did not attempt to contact defendant's counsel to follow up regarding whether defendant would address plaintiff's discovery concerns until June 24, 2015. In that e-mail communication, plaintiff's counsel sought the agreement of defendant's counsel regarding the timing of a motion to compel. Although plaintiff's counsel, having returned to the country by then, did respond to that communication the next day, plaintiff filed its motion to compel on June 25, 2015, and did so even though there remained some weeks before the motion was due. Under the circumstances, plaintiff did not act reasonably in bringing the motion before defendant's counsel was able to respond to plaintiff's counsel's June 24 e-mail. For that reason, sanctions are not appropriate and are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DORICKO VS. TSI SEMICONDUCTORS AMERICA, LLC A DELAWARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Filing Notice Of Appeal Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF JOHN DORICKO'S Motion For Order Filing Notice Of Appeal Nunc Pro Tunc. The motion is granted. The court orders that the notice of appeal is deemed filed on April 3, 2015, nunc pro tunc. The April 3, 2015, filing is timely pursuant to Schoutens v. Sup. Crt. (1950) 97 Cal.App.2d 855, 857. The court has jurisdiction to issue the nunc pro tunc order. "A document is 'filed' when it is actually delivered to the clerk of the court during office hours, even if the clerk erroneously refuses to file it." Eliceche v. Federal Land Bank Assoc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 1349, 1361. The court can reasonably infer that the clerk erroneously rejected the filing because it lacked a cover sheet. See CRC 3.220. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
